description_long: A cluster of five stylized mushrooms is depicted, each featuring a thick, cylindrical stem and a wide, domed cap. The caps are a vibrant red color adorned with evenly spaced white spots, reminiscent of the classic Amanita muscaria appearance. The stems are a pale beige at the base, transitioning to a darker brown near the cap, giving a two-toned effect. The mushrooms are grouped closely together, with their caps slightly overlapping, creating a dense, natural arrangement. The surface of the caps appears matte and slightly textured, while the stems are smooth and solid. No gills are visible beneath the caps, and the mushrooms rest on an undefined, neutral background.
description: A cluster of five red-capped, white-spotted mushrooms with thick, two-toned stems, closely grouped together. The caps are domed and matte, with no visible gills underneath.
description_view_0: Five mushrooms with red, white-spotted caps and thick stems, viewed from a slight angle showing the cluster's density and overlapping caps.
description_view_1: The cluster is seen more from the front, highlighting the central mushroom and the even spacing of the white spots on the caps.
description_view_2: A side angle reveals the rounded shape of the caps and the close proximity of the stems, with the cap pattern clearly visible.
description_view_3: Viewed from the opposite side, the arrangement of the mushrooms remains dense, and the two-toned stems are prominent.
